paper_title: EFT Pathways to Baryon $\bm\chi$PT: Chiral Construction of $\bm|\Delta B| =2

perturbations.why_wrong: The paper title and abstract focus on dinucleon decay and neutron-antineutron oscillations, which are $|\Delta B|=2$ processes. Changing this to $|\Delta B|=1$ contradicts the subsequent discussion of six-quark operators and dinucleon decay widths.

The abstract states the framework is for studying specific interactions; $|\Delta B| \neq 2$ is too broad and contradicts the specific focus on neutron-antineutron oscillations and dinucleon decay mentioned later in the abstract.

The text references Sakharov's conditions for baryon asymmetry. Sakharov's conditions explicitly require both C and CP violation; replacing CP with P (parity) is a violation of the fundamental theoretical requirements for baryogenesis cited in the reference.

The context explicitly links this transition to 'proton decay'. Proton decay is a $|\Delta B|=1$ process. Labeling it as $|\Delta B|=2$ creates an internal inconsistency with the definition of proton decay and the subsequent contrast with 'complementary' $|\Delta B|=2$ processes.

The text identifies the 'most iconic realization' as neutron-antineutron oscillations. A neutron ($B=1$) transitioning to an antineutron ($B=-1$) is mathematically a $\Delta B = -2$ (magnitude 2) process, not 6.

The sign of the mass term in the QCD Lagrangian is flipped from negative to positive. This is inconsistent with the standard definition of the fermion mass term and the subsequent discussion of the chiral limit $M \to 0$ where the mass term should subtract from the kinetic energy in the action.

The subscripts L and R are swapped in the representation assignments. This contradicts the definition of $q_R$ and $q_L$ transforming under $SU(3)_R$ and $SU(3)_L$ respectively, as established in the preceding text.
Parity transformation must swap chirality (L to R and R to L). By keeping the subscripts the same on both sides, the transformation fails to represent the physical action of parity on Weyl spinors.

The dimension of the quark condensate $\langle \bar{q}q \rangle$ must be [mass]^3. Changing the exponent of the chiral scale $\Lambda_\chi$ from 3 to 2 creates a dimensional inconsistency in the field theory definition.

The tensor $T^{SSS}$ is explicitly defined as being 'fully symmetric' in the text preceding the equation. Changing a '+' to a '-' in the sum of epsilon products violates the symmetry under the exchange of indices $\alpha \leftrightarrow \beta$.

The text defines the symmetrization and antisymmetrization conventions as $2\psi_{[a}\chi_{b]} = \psi_a\chi_b - \psi_b\chi_a$. Removing the 1/2 factor in the definition of the anti-symmetric tensor $\cX_{\repbar3}$ makes it inconsistent with the standard normalization of the epsilon tensor used for the $\repbar3$ representation in this basis.
The text immediately following the equation states 'The five dimension-nine operators relevant for our analysis are given by...', which contradicts the summation index $i=1$ to $6$.

The transformation of the Lagrangian from the SMEFT basis to the LEFT basis involves a linear combination of Wilson coefficients. The factor 1/2 in the first term is inconsistent with the 1/4 factors in the subsequent terms derived from the same color-tensor identity decomposition mentioned in the text.
